saves-menu.rename-content: You are about to rename save:
saves-menu.delete-content: Are you sure, you want to delete save:

first-lesson.dial1-1: To add a library to the program we use #include<library>
first-lesson.dial1-2: Everything inside the "main" function will be executed by the program after compilation.
first-lesson.dial1-3: std::cout << "hello world" command, makes the text inside double-quotes appear on the screen.
first-lesson.dial1-4: At the end of every line, with some exeptions, there needs to be a semicolon.
first-lesson.dial1-5: It's not necessary to use std:: when you add using namespace std at the very top of your code
first-lesson.dial2-1: You don't have to use std::, if you add using namespace std at the top of your code.
first-lesson.dial3-1: Using the program above you can assign values to two variables and display their sum.
first-lesson.dial3-2: With the help of variables, we can store some data, which can be used later in the program
first-lesson.dial3-3: There are multiple variable types, storing diffrent types of data:
first-lesson.dial3-5: float stores numbers with decimal places
first-lesson.dial3-8: bool stores a logical data - true or false
first-lesson.dial4-1: If statement allows to execute some code under a certain condition, which we put inside the brackets
first-lesson.dial4-2: The code above shows "x > 0", if value of variable x is greater that zero

messages.too-young: You are too young to drive a car!
